summary:
Root-cause  
When the feature flag features.enable_metrics is true but the metrics block is missing in config.json, cfg.Metrics remains nil.  
makeStatusHandler dereferences cfg.Metrics.Endpoint / cfg.Metrics.IntervalSec unconditionally, so the first request to /status panics with a nil-pointer and the tests fail.

Fix  
Give LoadConfig a safety-net: if EnableMetrics is true and Metrics is still nil, create a sensible default struct so the rest of the code can dereference it safely.

config.go (LoadConfig):

```go
// After applying basic defaults
if cfg.Features.EnableMetrics && cfg.Metrics == nil {
    cfg.Metrics = &MetricsConfig{
        Endpoint:    "/metrics",
        IntervalSec: 60,
    }
}
```

With this small addition the server always has a non-nil MetricsConfig whenever metrics are enabled. All endpoints work and test.sh now prints:

```
PASS: Server starts and responds correctly with metrics enabled
```

The “needle” (nil-pointer bug) has been removed.
